findings.summary: the fix did not reach repos that HAVE the defect: `missing` diffs path lines but not the comment, so an upgrade left the false header intact and appended a corrected stanza below it — two contradictory stanzas with the false claim first, on the path every existing consumer takes. Generator now heals in place; verified live on a mixed .gitignore and via git check-ignore

the new tests asserted substrings, which match a COMMENTED-OUT path — prefixing every emitted path with '# ' yields a stanza that ignores nothing and passed 3/3. Added a test that git-inits a temp dir and asserts via git check-ignore; reviewer independently probed it fails safe when init writes nothing (7 red)

REPORT-ONLY, cross-repo: the plugin's vendored engine bundle still carries the old stanza, so `vfkb init` AS DELIVERED still emits both defects until re-vendored and released. Named in the commit and PR body rather than claimed fixed (ADR-0051 §2)
findIndex heals only the FIRST stale header; a hand-edited file with two would need a second run. Never emitted by the generator
a stale header at EOF loses the trailing newline in the heal branch; git parses it fine
the heal rewrites CRLF files to LF, producing whole-file churn for a Windows consumer; functionally correct

the false claim also survives in CLAUDE.md:80, docs/CONSUMER-ONBOARDING*.md, docs/RUNBOOK-claude-code-integration.md and the plugin's own .gitignore — deliberately out of scope for a 2-file generator fix; follow-up filed
